question: Whose death is recorded at the very beginning of 1 Samuel 25?
explanation: Samuel the prophet died and all Israel gathered together to mourn for him. He was buried at his home in Ramah. Samuel was a very important leader in Israel who anointed both Saul and David as kings!

question: After Samuel's death, where did David go?
explanation: David went down to the Wilderness of Paran. This was a big desert area in the south. David was still running from King Saul, so he had to keep moving to stay safe!

question: Nabal was a descendant of which famous Old Testament figure?
explanation: Nabal was of the house of Caleb! Caleb was one of the two faithful spies who trusted God when Israel first came to the Promised Land. Too bad Nabal didn't inherit Caleb's good attitude!

question: What was Nabal doing in Carmel when David sent his young men?
explanation: Nabal was shearing his sheep in Carmel. Sheep-shearing time was like a harvest festival -- a time of celebration and generosity. That's why David thought it was a good time to ask for provisions!

question: What greeting did David instruct his young men to give Nabal?
options: "Peace be to you, peace to your house, and peace to all that you have"
"The Lord bless you and keep you"
"May the Lord be with your spirit"
explanation: David told his men to say 'Peace be to you, peace to your house, and peace to all that you have!' That's a triple peace blessing! David was being super polite and respectful to Nabal.

question: David told his young men to go to Nabal and greet him in whose name?
options: In the name of the Lord
In the name of King Saul
explanation: David told the young men to greet Nabal 'in my name' -- that is, in David's name. This was a formal and respectful way to make the request. David wanted Nabal to know exactly who was asking!

question: How many of David's men stayed behind with the supplies while 400 went to fight Nabal?
explanation: Two hundred men stayed with the supplies while 400 went with David. That means David had about 600 men total! Somebody had to guard all their stuff while the rest went on the mission.

question: Did Abigail tell Nabal she was going to meet David with provisions?
options: Yes, but only after she packed the donkeys
She sent a servant to tell him later
explanation: Abigail did NOT tell her husband Nabal! The Bible says she told her servants to go ahead and she would follow, 'but she did not tell her husband Nabal.' She knew he would have stopped her wise plan!

question: What animal did Abigail ride when she went to meet David?
explanation: Abigail rode on a donkey as she went down under cover of the hill to meet David. Donkeys were very common for travel in Bible times, and they were great for going through rocky, hilly paths!

question: What oath did Abigail use when she spoke to David, beginning with what phrase?
options: "By the name of the Lord God of Israel"
"As the Lord lives and as your soul lives"
"By the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob"
explanation: Abigail said 'As the LORD lives and as your soul lives.' This was a very serious oath in Bible times! Abigail was making a solemn promise to David, showing how seriously she took the whole situation.

question: Abigail told David that his life would be 'bound in the bundle of the living with the LORD your God.' What did she say would happen to his enemies' lives?
options: They would be scattered like dust
They would be cut off from the land
God would sling them out as from the pocket of a sling
They would melt like wax before fire
explanation: Abigail said God would 'sling out' the lives of David's enemies, 'as from the pocket of a sling!' This was a clever reference to how David defeated Goliath with a sling. Abigail was one smart lady!

question: What request did Abigail make of David for the future?
options: That he would spare Nabal's life
That he would protect her household
That he would remember her when the Lord dealt well with him
That he would make peace with King Saul
explanation: Abigail asked David to remember her when God blessed him. She could see that David would become a great leader one day! And sure enough, David did remember her and later sent for her to be his wife.

question: Approximately how many days after Nabal's feast did the Lord strike him and he died?
explanation: It was about ten days later that the Lord struck Nabal and he died. First his heart 'died within him and he became like a stone,' and then about ten days after that, the Lord struck him. God took care of the situation in His own time!

question: After Nabal died, how did David propose to Abigail?
options: He went to her house personally
He sent his servants to her to propose marriage
He sent a letter through a messenger
He met her at the well in the city
explanation: David sent servants to Abigail to speak to her and propose marriage on his behalf. When Abigail heard David's proposal, she bowed down and quickly got ready to go with his servants. What a love story!

question: In 1 Samuel 25:3, what two qualities are specifically used to describe Abigail alongside her connection to Nabal?
options: Wise in heart and gracious in speech
explanation: The Bible says Abigail was 'a woman of good understanding and beautiful appearance.' She had both brains AND beauty! The verse then contrasts her with Nabal who was 'harsh and evil in his doings.' What a difference between husband and wife!

question: Why did the Lord allow the Canaanites to oppress Israel before Deborah became judge?
options: The children of Israel did evil in the sight of the Lord
Israel made a treaty with Egypt
Israel stopped going to the tabernacle
explanation: The children of Israel again did evil in the sight of the Lord! This is a pattern you see again and again in Judges -- Israel sins, God allows an enemy to oppress them, they cry out to God, and God raises up a judge to save them.

question: Which judge had died before Deborah's story begins in Judges 4?
explanation: Ehud had died before the events of Judges 4. Ehud was the left-handed judge who defeated King Eglon of Moab. After Ehud died, Israel went back to doing evil, and that's when God allowed King Jabin to oppress them.

question: What was the name of the city where Sisera, the commander of Jabin's army, dwelt?
explanation: Sisera dwelt in Harosheth Hagoyim, which means 'Harosheth of the Gentiles.' That's quite a fancy name for a city! Sisera was the powerful commander of King Jabin's army with all those 900 iron chariots.

question: How did Deborah summon Barak to come to her?
options: She sent and called for him
He came on his own to seek advice
She called out to him from her palm tree
explanation: Deborah SENT and called for Barak son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali. She didn't go to him -- she summoned him! That shows how much authority Deborah had as a judge and prophetess in Israel.

question: What did Deborah say to Barak when she gave him God's command? She began with which phrase?
options: "Thus says the Lord God of Israel"
"Has not the Lord God of Israel commanded"
"The Lord your God says to you"
explanation: Deborah said, 'Has not the LORD God of Israel commanded?' She was reminding Barak that this wasn't just her idea -- it was GOD's command! Deborah spoke with the full authority of God's word.

question: Which two tribes did Deborah specifically tell Barak to gather men from?
explanation: Deborah told Barak to take men from the tribes of Naphtali and Zebulun. These were tribes in the northern part of Israel, right near where the battle would take place. God had a specific plan for which soldiers to use!

question: Where did Deborah tell Barak to gather the 10,000 men?
explanation: Mount Tabor was the gathering place! It's a big mountain in northern Israel that rises up from a flat valley. It was a great strategic location because from up there, you could see the enemy coming from far away.

question: According to Judges 4:15, what did the Lord do to Sisera and his army?
options: The Lord routed Sisera and all his chariots and all his army
The Lord sent fire from heaven
explanation: The LORD routed Sisera and all his chariots and army with the edge of the sword before Barak. 'Routed' means He threw them into total panic and confusion! God did the real fighting -- Barak and his men just had to show up and be brave.

question: After the Lord routed his army, what did Sisera do?
options: He alighted from his chariot and fled away on foot
He hid among the dead soldiers
explanation: Sisera jumped down from his chariot and ran away on foot! Imagine that -- the mighty commander of 900 iron chariots was so terrified that he abandoned his own chariot and ran! That's what happens when God fights the battle.

question: What exact words did Jael say to invite Sisera into her tent?
options: "Come in, come in, do not be afraid"
"Turn aside, my lord, turn aside to me; do not fear"
"Enter and rest, my lord, for you are safe here"
"Welcome, my lord, come and hide yourself"
explanation: Jael said, 'Turn aside, my lord, turn aside to me; do not fear.' She sounded so welcoming and reassuring! Sisera thought he was going to a safe hiding place, but God had a very different plan in store for him.

question: What did Jael use to cover Sisera when he lay down in her tent?
explanation: Jael covered Sisera with a rug (some translations say blanket). She was making him feel safe and comfortable so he would fall asleep. Sisera was exhausted from running and thought he had found a perfect hiding spot!

question: What did Sisera ask Jael for to drink?
explanation: Sisera asked Jael, 'Please give me a little water to drink, for I am thirsty.' But Jael did even better -- she opened a jug of milk and gave him a drink instead! Then she covered him up again. She was being extra hospitable before her big move!

question: After Jael defeated Sisera, who came pursuing him, and what did Jael do?
options: Barak came, and Jael went out to show him Sisera
Joshua came, and Jael told him the news
Gideon came, and Jael called to him
explanation: Barak came in pursuit of Sisera, and Jael went out to meet him and said, 'Come, I will show you the man whom you seek.' When Barak went in, he found Sisera lying dead! Deborah's prophecy came true -- the glory went to a woman, not to Barak.

question: Who sang the victory song together in Judges chapter 5?
explanation: Deborah and Barak sang together on that day! Their song is one of the most famous victory songs in the entire Bible. It praises God for the amazing victory He gave Israel over Sisera and his powerful army.

question: In the Song of Deborah, what place is mentioned in the phrase 'Lord, when You went out from Seir, when You marched from the field of Edom'?
explanation: The song says 'LORD, when You went out from Seir, when You marched from the field of Edom.' Seir and Edom refer to the same region south of Israel. The song is remembering how God powerfully led His people from the very beginning!

question: What is the exact repeated rallying cry directed at Deborah in Judges 5:12?
options: "Rise up, rise up, Deborah! Rise up and lead!"
"Be strong, be strong, Deborah! Be strong and courageous!"
"Awake, awake, Deborah! Awake, awake, sing a song!"
"Stand firm, stand firm, Deborah! Stand firm in the Lord!"
explanation: The song cries out, 'Awake, awake, Deborah! Awake, awake, sing a song!' The word 'awake' is repeated four times! It's like an excited cheer rallying Deborah to rise up and celebrate what God has done. How exciting!

question: In the Song of Deborah, which tribes are criticized for NOT coming to fight in the battle?
explanation: Reuben, Dan, and Asher are called out for staying behind! Reuben stayed with their sheep, Dan remained on their ships, and Asher stayed by the seashore. When God calls you to action, you don't want to be the one who stayed home!

question: In Judges 5, who is described as looking through a window, waiting and wondering?
explanation: The mother of Sisera looked through the window and cried out, wondering where her son was. She kept waiting for his chariot to come home. It's a sad picture -- she didn't know her son would never return from the battle.

question: Zacharias the priest belonged to which priestly division?
explanation: Zacharias was of the division of Abijah. King David had organized the priests into 24 divisions for serving in the temple, and Abijah's division was the eighth one. Each division took turns serving in the temple!

question: Which king was ruling when the story of Zacharias and Elizabeth begins?
explanation: It was 'in the days of Herod, the king of Judea.' This was Herod the Great, who was known for being a very cruel king. But even in dark times under a bad king, God was working out His amazing plan!

question: How does Luke 1:6 describe Zacharias and Elizabeth's character?
options: They were righteous before God, walking blameless in all the commandments
They were humble and poor in spirit
They were faithful in tithing and prayer
explanation: They were 'both righteous before God, walking in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blameless.' What an amazing description! They truly loved God and obeyed His laws with all their hearts.

question: Why did Zacharias and Elizabeth have no children?
options: They had not been married long
They chose not to have children
explanation: Elizabeth was barren, which means she couldn't have children, and they were both well advanced in years. But God loves to do the impossible! Just like He did with Sarah and Hannah, God had a miracle baby planned for Elizabeth too.

question: How was Zacharias chosen to burn incense in the temple?
options: His lot fell to burn incense
He was next in the rotation schedule
explanation: His lot fell to burn incense! The priests cast lots (like drawing straws) to decide who would do this special job. A priest might only get this honor once in his lifetime, so this was a HUGE deal for Zacharias!

question: What were the first words the angel Gabriel said to Zacharias?
explanation: Gabriel said, 'Do not be afraid, Zacharias!' Zacharias was troubled and afraid when he saw the angel. 'Do not be afraid' is something angels say a LOT in the Bible -- because seeing an angel is pretty scary!

question: According to the angel, John would be great in whose sight?
options: In the sight of all Israel
In the sight of kings and rulers
In the sight of the Lord
In the sight of the nations
explanation: John would 'be great in the sight of the Lord.' It doesn't matter if the whole world thinks you're important -- what matters most is being great in GOD's eyes! And John was exactly that.

question: What did the angel say John must never drink?
explanation: The angel said John 'shall drink neither wine nor strong drink.' This was similar to a Nazirite vow, showing that John was set apart for God's special purpose from before he was even born! He was dedicated completely to God.

question: From when would John be filled with the Holy Spirit, according to the angel?
options: From the day of his circumcision
explanation: John would be 'filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother's womb!' That's incredible -- filled with God's Spirit before he was even born! We already saw this happen when baby John leaped in Elizabeth's womb when Mary visited.

question: The angel said John would go before the Lord in the spirit and power of which Old Testament prophet?
explanation: John would go in 'the spirit and power of Elijah!' Just like Elijah boldly called Israel to repent, John the Baptist would do the same thing. He would prepare the way for Jesus by turning people's hearts back to God.

question: According to Luke 1:17, what was John's mission described as?
options: To make ready a people prepared for the Lord
To rebuild the temple of God
To restore the kingdom to Israel
explanation: John's mission was 'to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.' He was like an advance team getting everything ready before the King arrives! John's whole life purpose was to prepare people's hearts for Jesus.

question: When Zacharias asked how he could be sure of the angel's message, what did Gabriel say about himself?
options: "I am a messenger of the Most High"
"I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God"
"I am sent from the throne of heaven"
"I am the angel of the Lord Almighty"
explanation: Gabriel said, 'I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God!' Wow -- Gabriel actually stands right in the presence of God Himself! When an angel like that brings you a message, you'd better believe it. Poor Zacharias learned that the hard way!

question: Where was the angel sent to visit Mary?
options: To Nazareth, a city of Galilee
To Capernaum by the Sea of Galilee
explanation: Gabriel was sent to Nazareth, a city of Galilee. Nazareth was a small, unimportant town -- people even said 'Can anything good come out of Nazareth?' But God chose this humble place for the most amazing announcement in history!

question: What were Gabriel's exact first words to Mary in the NKJV?
options: "Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with you"
"Rejoice, highly favored one, the Lord is with you"
"Greetings, blessed one, the Lord has chosen you"
"Peace to you, favored daughter of Israel"
explanation: Gabriel said, 'Rejoice, highly favored one, the Lord is with you; blessed are you among women!' What an amazing greeting! Mary was chosen by God out of all the women in the world for the most special job ever.

question: According to Luke 1:35, how did the angel describe what would happen to Mary? Complete: 'The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the _____ will overshadow you.'
explanation: The NKJV says 'the power of the Highest will overshadow you.' This was how God would perform the miracle of Jesus being born -- through the power of the Holy Spirit and the Highest (God Himself). It was a one-of-a-kind miracle!

question: What famous phrase did the angel say to Mary about God's power?
options: "The arm of the Lord is not shortened"
explanation: 'For with God nothing will be impossible!' This is one of the most encouraging verses in the whole Bible. No matter how big your problem seems, remember -- NOTHING is impossible with God. He can do anything!

question: What was Mary's response to the angel's message about becoming the mother of Jesus?
options: "I will do as you say, my lord"
"Let it be to me according to your word"
"May the will of God be done"
"I accept this with a grateful heart"
explanation: Mary said, 'Behold the maidservant of the Lord! Let it be to me according to your word.' What incredible faith and humility! Mary didn't argue or question anymore -- she simply trusted God and said YES to His plan.

question: How did Mary travel to visit Elizabeth after the angel's announcement?
options: She went slowly with a caravan
She arose and went with haste into the hill country to a city of Judah
She waited a month then journeyed south
She traveled by donkey with Joseph
explanation: Mary 'arose in those days and went with haste into the hill country, to a city of Judah.' She didn't waste any time -- she hurried to see her cousin Elizabeth! Mary was so excited about what God was doing that she rushed to share it.

question: At the cross, what were Jesus's exact words to Mary about the beloved disciple?
options: "Dear mother, he will care for you"
"Woman, this disciple will be your son"
explanation: Jesus said, 'Woman, behold your son!' Even while suffering on the cross, Jesus cared about His mother's future. He made sure she would be taken care of by the disciple He loved. What an amazing example of love and responsibility!

question: Mary was betrothed (engaged) to which man when the angel Gabriel visited her?
explanation: Mary was betrothed to a man named Joseph, who was of the house of David. Being betrothed was like being engaged but even more serious in Bible times. God chose Joseph to be the earthly father who would help raise baby Jesus!
